# servicenow_email_template (Resource)

`servicenow_email_template` manages an email template (sysevent_email_template) within ServiceNow.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Manages an email template (sysevent_email_template) in ServiceNow.
resource "servicenow_email_template" "example" {
  name         = "Example Incident Template"
  table        = "incident"
  subject      = "Incident ${number} update"
  message_html = "<p>Incident has been updated.</p>"
  description  = "Reusable email template for incident updates."
  active       = true
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) Name of the email template.

### Optional

- `message_html` (String) HTML body of the email template.
- `message_plain` (String) Plain text body of the email template (maps to ServiceNow's `message` column).
- `protection_policy` (String) Determines how application files are protected when downloaded or installed. Can be empty for no protection, 'read' for read-only protection or 'protected'.
- `scope` (String) Associates a resource to a specific application ID in ServiceNow.
- `sms_alternate` (String) SMS alternate text for the email template.
- `subject` (String) Subject line for the email template.
- `table` (String) Table that the email template is associated with (maps to ServiceNow's `collection` column).

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# Import using the sys_id of the record in ServiceNow.
terraform import servicenow_email_template.example <sys_id>
```
